# UC-17.1.53 · Posture Remediation Funnel — Detection-to-Compliant Conversion Rate

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When ISE finds a sick computer (missing antivirus, missing patch), it tries to fix it before letting it on the network. We measure how many sick computers actually got fixed — the lower the number, the more useless the fix-up rules are.*

---

## Description

Tracks the posture-remediation funnel: how many endpoints flagged as non-compliant successfully reach a compliant state, broken down per posture policy. Exposes ineffective posture policies (low conversion rate) for tuning.

## Value

Posture is only valuable if non-compliant endpoints actually become compliant. A posture policy with a 20% conversion rate is largely ineffective — endpoints stay quarantined or fall through default-allow. Tracking the funnel weekly drives policy improvement.

## Implementation

Confirm *Posture* category at INFO. Schedule weekly summary report (Monday 06:00 UTC). For real-time per-endpoint tracking, schedule a 4h cadence search to populate a summary index `ise_posture_funnel` with per-endpoint state.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger posture remediation on 10 lab endpoints; 8 succeed, 2 fail. UC-17.1.53 reports the policy with `endpoints=10, compliant=8, conversion_pct=80`.

### Negative scenario

All 10 succeed. UC-17.1.53 reports `conversion_pct=100`.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE Posture configured.
- Posture agent deployed to endpoints.
- `posture_policies.csv` with `posture_policy`, `target_conversion_pct`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Posture* category at INFO.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Weekly search in `spl` field.

A secondary 4h cadence search populates summary index for trend analysis.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Posture Funnel":
- Funnel chart per policy.
- Top-N lowest conversion.
- Trend over 12 weeks.

Weekly review: posture team reviews lowest-conversion policies and tunes.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **All policies show 0% conversion** — Posture agent not reaching endpoints; cross-ref UC-17.1.54.
- **One policy at 0%** — Remediation action mis-configured.
- **Trend declining** — New endpoint type incompatible with existing remediation.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (15042, 15044, 15045, 15046) earliest=-7d
| eval stage = case(MessageCode=="15042", "detected", MessageCode=="15044", "remediation_started", MessageCode=="15045", "remediation_completed", MessageCode=="15046", "remediation_failed")
| stats values(stage) as stages dc(_time) as event_count by endpoint_mac, posture_policy
| eval reached_compliance = if(mvfind(stages, "remediation_completed")>=0, "yes", "no")
| stats count as endpoints, sum(eval(if(reached_compliance=="yes",1,0))) as compliant by posture_policy
| eval conversion_pct = round((compliant/endpoints)*100, 1)
| sort conversion_pct
```

## Visualization

(1) Funnel chart: detected → remediation_started → remediation_completed. (2) Top-N: lowest-conversion-rate posture policies. (3) Trend: weekly conversion rate per policy.

## Known False Positives

- **First-day rollout** — A new posture policy may show low conversion until devices receive the agent and remediation packages.
- **Endpoint offline** — An endpoint that goes offline mid-remediation appears as non-converted; track only endpoints currently online.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Posture Services](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
